data.title: Telix Pharmaceuticals: Balance Sheet De-Risking and Clinical Momentum Support Further Upside and Buy Rating
data.description:
Telix Pharmaceuticals, the Healthcare sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst David Bailey from Morgan Stanley maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a A$24.60 price target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Bailey has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Telix’s proactive balance sheet management and robust operating momentum. The company’s new US$550 mn convertible bond issue is being used to retire existing 2029 notes, alleviating investor concerns around the 2027 put feature and reducing a key technical overhang on the stock.

In addition, Telix has delivered a solid 1Q26 performance, resubmitted the Pixclara NDA, and entered into a strategic collaboration with Regeneron, all of which strengthen its growth outlook. Positive progress in the Phase 3 ProstACT Global trial for TLX591 and a risk‑weighted valuation that already supports levels around the current share price underpin Bailey’s view that further upside remains, justifying a Buy recommendation.

Bailey covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as Telix Pharmaceuticals, Ramsay Health Care, and Resmed. According to TipRanks, Bailey has an average return of 8.8% and a 44.79% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released yesterday, Citi also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a A$32.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: GFL Environmental: Strategic Expansion via Secure Waste Acquisition Supports Buy Rating and Upside to C$60 Target
data.description:
Jon Windham, an analyst from UBS, maintained the Buy rating on GFL Environmental. The associated price target is $60.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Jon Windham has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to GFL’s strategic positioning and valuation. He views the planned acquisition of Secure Waste Infrastructure as materially expanding GFL’s scale and cash flow base, with Secure Waste poised to represent a meaningful share of consolidated EBITDA once integrated.

At the same time, he acknowledges that traditional municipal solid waste investors may initially react cautiously to the added energy exposure and cyclicality. Nonetheless, his C$60 price target, supported by a premium EV/EBITDA multiple and additional value from GFL’s stake in the partially divested Environmental Services unit, implies attractive upside from current levels, justifying the Buy recommendation.

data.title: Premium-Priced Secure Deal Heightens Volatility and Leverage Risk, Supporting Sell Rating on GFL
data.description:
J.P. Morgan analyst Tami Zakaria downgraded the rating on GFL Environmental to a Sell today, setting a price target of $42.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Tami Zakaria has given his Sell rating due to a combination of factors related to valuation, deal structure, and business mix. He argues that GFL is paying a rich price for Secure Waste relative to prior transactions and the target’s historical trading range, without clear evidence of outsized synergies that would justify the premium multiple. The expected contribution from energy and production (E&P) waste operations also introduces greater earnings volatility and oil-price sensitivity, which he believes should command a lower valuation multiple than GFL’s core solid waste business.

In addition, Zakaria highlights that the combined company will have a weaker margin profile and higher leverage than larger solid waste peers, which in his view merits a discount rather than the current valuation. He notes that net leverage is likely to remain above 3x after the acquisition and that GFL’s like-for-like EBITDA margins lag best-in-class competitors, all at a time when interest-rate cuts are not guaranteed. As a result, he sees the risk-reward as skewed to the downside and expects the Secure transaction to act as a catalyst for a re-rating lower, supporting his Underweight recommendation and $42 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
